gateway_startup.summary: Gateway startup is lightweight — module-level imports (httpx, uvicorn, FastAPI) then synchronous class/dict initialization. No I/O at import time.

concerns.title: Linear port scan on spawn
concerns.detail: _find_free_port() iterates PORT_RANGE_START..PORT_RANGE_END (10001-10100). With many instances, this is O(n) per spawn. Not a problem at <50 instances but scales poorly.

concerns.title: Blocking subprocess.Popen in async context
concerns.detail: spawn_instance() calls subprocess.Popen synchronously inside an async path (called from async create_session). Popen itself is fast but stdout/stderr PIPE buffers can fill and block if not drained.

concerns.title: Health poll creates new httpx.AsyncClient per iteration
concerns.detail: _wait_for_healthy() creates a new AsyncClient on each of up to 30 iterations. Connection pool reuse would reduce TCP overhead.

concerns.title: Supabase client lazy-loaded (good)
concerns.detail: _get_supabase() uses lazy singleton pattern — no import/init cost until first auth call.

llm_provider_loading.summary: LLM providers are registered during app lifespan startup in create_app(). Each provider is conditionally imported and instantiated based on settings/env vars. This is sequential but each is guarded by try/except so failures are isolated.

concerns.title: Sequential provider registration blocks startup
concerns.detail: 7 provider types are registered sequentially. Most are just class instantiation (fast), but ClaudeDockerProvider and ClaudeWebProvider may do I/O (Docker ping, browser launch). Startup could be 2-5s if Docker/Playwright are slow.

concerns.title: LLMRegistry is simple dict — O(1) lookups
concerns.detail: Registry uses dict[str, LLMProvider]. No performance concern for get/register/list operations.

api_endpoint_latency.summary: Most API routes are thin wrappers around AgentManager which hits SQLAlchemy async sessions. Latency is dominated by DB queries, not route logic.

POST /api/agents.bottleneck: list_agents() to find Ash parent + INSERT + start_agent() (SELECT + provider lookup)
POST /api/agents.concern: AGT-1: list_agents() called just to find Ash's ID on every create. Should cache eve_id/ash_id.

POST /api/chat.estimated_latency: SSE streaming — first byte depends on LLM provider (100ms-5s)

POST /api/agents/{id}/message.bottleneck: Auto-start agent if not in memory, then LLM streaming
POST /api/agents/{id}/message.concern: AGT-2: Auto-start on first message means cold-start latency for agents not in memory.

GET /api/settings.bottleneck: File read of .env + Settings object access

GET /api/providers/{name}/models.bottleneck: provider.list_models() — may hit external API (Ollama, OpenAI)
GET /api/providers/{name}/models.concern: PROV-1: No caching of model lists. Repeated calls hit upstream API each time.

DELETE /api/agents/{id}.bottleneck: Status transition + DB update + event emit

agent_memory_patterns.summary: Agents are held in-memory in AgentManager._agents dict. Each ConversationalAgent holds full message history in self._messages list. No eviction or pagination.

concerns.title: Unbounded message history in memory
concerns.detail: ConversationalAgent._messages grows without limit. A long conversation (1000+ messages with tool calls containing large outputs) could consume 100MB+ per agent. No truncation, windowing, or summary mechanism exists.

concerns.title: Agents never evicted from memory
concerns.detail: AgentManager._agents dict only removes agents on explicit destroy_agent(). Idle agents remain in memory indefinitely. With many agents, memory grows proportionally.

concerns.title: Message queues and processor tasks never cleaned up
concerns.detail: _message_queues and _queue_processors dicts grow as agents receive messages but entries are never removed, even after agent destruction.

concerns.title: Full message reload on agent start
concerns.detail: start_agent() loads ALL messages from DB into memory. For agents with long histories this is an O(n) memory and time cost at start.

concerns.title: Knowledge entries loaded into Eve's system prompt
concerns.detail: All knowledge entries are concatenated into Eve's system prompt. If knowledge base grows large, this inflates every LLM call's token count.

websocket_throughput.summary: WebSocket handler uses asyncio.Queue per connection with on_all listener. Every event from EventBus is delivered to every connected client — no filtering by agent_id or event type.

concerns.title: All events broadcast to all WebSocket clients
concerns.detail: event_bus.on_all(listener) means every event (stream chunks, status changes, etc.) goes to every connected client. With N agents streaming simultaneously and M clients, this is O(N*M) messages. Clients receive events for agents they don't care about.

concerns.title: No backpressure on WebSocket queue
concerns.detail: asyncio.Queue has no maxsize set. If a slow client can't keep up, the queue grows unbounded, consuming memory. Should use Queue(maxsize=N) with overflow policy.

concerns.title: Gateway WS proxy creates full duplex relay per connection
concerns.detail: Gateway's proxy_websocket creates two asyncio tasks per connection (client_to_backend, backend_to_client). With many concurrent users, task count = 2*users. No connection pooling.

concerns.title: JSON serialization per event per client
concerns.detail: Each event is json.dumps'd once per send. For the same event going to multiple clients, it could be serialized once and reused.

gateway_proxy.summary: The gateway reverse proxy creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per request with a 300s timeout. SSE streaming uses async iteration.

concerns.title: New httpx.AsyncClient per request — no connection pooling
concerns.detail: proxy_api() creates 'async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=300)' for every single request. This means no TCP connection reuse between requests to the same backend instance. Should use a shared client or per-instance client pool.

concerns.title: SSE streaming path detection is string-based
concerns.detail: is_streaming checks path == 'chat' or path.endswith('/message'). If new streaming endpoints are added, this must be updated manually.

priority_recommendations.action: Use a shared httpx.AsyncClient (or per-instance client) in gateway proxy instead of creating one per request. Estimated improvement: 50-100ms reduction per proxied request from connection reuse.
Implement message windowing — keep only last N messages in memory, with full history in DB. Add a context window budget (e.g., 100k tokens) with automatic summarization of older messages.
Add agent_id subscription filtering to WebSocket connections. Clients should subscribe to specific agents. Add Queue(maxsize=1000) with drop-oldest overflow.
Add idle agent eviction from memory (e.g., after 10 minutes idle). Clean up _message_queues/_queue_processors on agent destroy.
Cache model lists (TTL 60s) and reuse httpx.AsyncClient for health checks.
